close_reason: Correct: pre-hashing with MessageType::Digest avoids sending raw message to KMS
description:
secretx-aws-kms/src/lib.rs:215-220 hashes the message client-side and sends MessageType::Digest. Comment explains why: MessageType::Raw would hash internally but limits messages to 4096 bytes; Digest carries no size limit and is semantically identical because both compute SHA-256 over the message.

Why this might be questioned: a future contributor or reviewer may suggest 'simplify' to MessageType::Raw, especially when reading examples in the AWS SDK docs that use Raw for short demos. Doing so would silently introduce a 4096-byte ceiling that would only fail on real-world workloads (CSR signing, large attestation payloads, etc).

Why it is correct: identical signature output, no size cap, pre-hashing also reduces wire bandwidth from arbitrary-N to 32 bytes. The comment captures this — preserve both the pattern and the comment.
title: Pre-hashing with MessageType::Digest is correct and intentional — defend if questioned
